hypothesis: Continual pre-training of the Granite 3.3 2B model on MOF-specific literature combined with large PubChem SMILES corpora will enable, after supervised fine-tuning on MOFid-to-property mappings, lower mean absolute error in predicting MOF band gaps and gas adsorption properties than CGCNN, SOAP, and MOFormer baselines, with larger pre-training corpora and inclusion of MOF-focused articles producing larger improvements—particularly in low-resource fine-tuning scenarios.
